title: Client vs Server
summary: The client is the device that asks for something, like your phone or browser.
The server is the computer that waits for requests and sends back answers.
They talk by sending requests and getting responses.
The same computer can be a client one moment and a server the next.

sections.text: When you open an app or a website, two computers are having a quick conversation. One asks for things, the other hands them over. Once you know who is who, the rest of the web starts to make sense.

media.alt: A phone asking a server for data and getting a reply

sections.title: What is a Client?
sections.text: The client is the device that asks for something. Your phone, your laptop, the browser you are reading this on. It sends a request that says please give me this page or this data, then waits for an answer.

sections.title: What is a Server?
sections.text: A server is a computer whose whole job is to wait for requests and answer them. It might store web pages, images, videos, or the data behind an app. It is usually always on, ready to reply the moment someone asks.

sections.title: A Customer and a Kitchen
sections.text: Think of a restaurant. You, the customer, are the client. You ask the waiter for a dish. The kitchen, the server, prepares it and sends it out. You never walk into the kitchen yourself. You just ask, and you receive.

media.alt: A customer ordering food while the kitchen prepares it

sections.title: How a Client and Server Talk
items: The client sends a request to the server.
The request says exactly what it wants, like a certain page.
The server reads the request and finds what was asked for.
The server sends a response back to the client.
The client takes that response and shows it to you.

sections.text: One computer can be both a client and a server at once. When a server needs data from another server, it becomes a client in that moment.

terms.definition: The device that asks for something, like your phone or browser.
A computer that waits for requests and sends back what was asked for.
A message asking for a page, an image, or some data.
The server's reply, carrying what the client asked for.
A building full of servers that stay on and connected all the time.

options: The device that asks for something
A computer that only sends, never asks
questions.explanation: The client is the device that makes the request, like your phone or your browser.

questions.text: What is a server's main job?
options: To wait for requests and answer them
To show pages on your screen
questions.explanation: A server waits for requests and sends back what was asked for, like a page or some data.

questions.text: In the restaurant example, who is the server?
questions.explanation: The kitchen plays the server. It receives the order and prepares what was asked for, just like a real server.
